HDFC Bank, Maruti Suzuki weigh on Indian benchmarks
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes fell on Thursday, weighed down by losses in heavyweight HDFC Bank and car maker Maruti Suzuki India, while uncertainty over U.S. tariffs continued to dent risk sentiment.

The Nifty 50 was down 0.16% at 22,896.30 points as of 11:01 a.m. IST, while the BSE Sensex lost 0.26% to 75,742.38.

The broader midcap and smallcap stocks were up 0.5% and 0.8%, respectively.

The Nifty 50 is likely to remain range-bound until there is some certainty about U.S. tariffs, said U.R. Bhat, co-founder of Alphaniti Fintech.

HDFC Bank , the heaviest stock on the benchmark indexes, slipped 2.1%, snapping a three-day winning streak. Shares of the private lender changed hands at a discount in multiple blocks on Thursday, data compiled by LSEG showed.

The sharp fall also led financials 0.8% lower.

Maruti Suzuki India fell 2% after its parent Suzuki Motor Corp said it planned to launch only four battery electric vehicles in India by fiscal year 2030 instead of the six targetted earlier.

Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump said on Tuesday he would introduce tariffs of 25% and above on autos, pharmaceuticals and semiconductors.

Among Asian countries, South Korea and Japan have the highest U.S. exposure to auto exports, while India has extensive pharma exposure, according to HSBC Global Research.

On Wednesday, the minutes of the U.S. Federal Reserve's January policy meeting showed that policymakers were concerned about the inflationary impact of Trump's tariff plans, signalling the likelihood of higher rates in the U.S.

Higher U.S. rates make emerging markets such as India unattractive for foreign investors, who have sold about $12.31 billion worth of Indian equities so far in 2025.

Among individual stocks, cigarette makers VST Industries and Godfrey Phillips fell 4% each, while ITC lost 1.2% on a report of a possible tax hike.

data.title: Dutch Jobless Rate Ticks Higher in January
data.description:
The Netherland’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate edged up to 3.8% in January 2025, up from 3.7% in the previous five months.

This marked the highest rate since September 2022, as the number of unemployed individuals increased by 13 thousand to 386 thousand, while employment rose by 1 thousand to 9.815 million.

The labor force participation rate ticked up to 76% from 75.9% in the prior period.

Meanwhile, there were 189 thousand current recipients of unemployment benefits during the period, up by 8.4% compared to December.

In January 2024, the seasonally adjusted jobless rate was at 3.6%.

Global Brands Manufacture Logs NT$2.84 Billion Profit in 2024
data.description:
Global Brands Manufacture posted a profit attributable to owners of NT$2.84 billion, or NT$6.01 per basic share, for 2024, according to a Tuesday Taiwan bourse filing.

The company did not provide comparative year-ago figures.

Operating revenue stood at NT$21.7 billion, the printed circuit assembly manufacturer said.

data.title: Zurich Insurance posts slight operating profit beat in 2024
data.description:
Zurich Insurance reported slightly higher than expected annual operating profit on Thursday, as insurers ride out the impact of climate disasters and wars.

Europe's fifth largest insurer said its operating profit was $7.8 billion last year, just ahead of analysts' estimate of $7.7 billion in a company-provided consensus.

ACWA Power's Minority-owned Saudi Solar Power Plant Starts Full Operations
data.description:
ACWA Power Co. (SASE:2082) said Thursday its partly-owned Al Shuaiba 2 solar power plant in Saudi Arabia became fully operational after receiving regulatory approvals.

The independent power plant, with a total capacity of 2,060 megawatts, received the commercial operational certificate from the Saudi Power Procurement Co.

The Saudi utility company owns a 35.01% stake in the project company.

data.title: US to cut size of diplomatic mission in China by up to 10%, SCMP reports
data.description:
The United States is set to cut the size of its diplomatic mission in China by up to 10%, the South China Morning Post (SCMP) reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the situation.

American diplomats working in mainland China and Hong Kong as well as local employees might be given notice as early as Friday in a round of downsizing that the media outlet described as unprecedented.

The cuts would affect the embassy in Beijing and the consulates in Guangzhou, Shanghai, Shenyang and Wuhan, as well as the consulate in Hong Kong, the SCMP said.

It was not clear whether any of those affected would be reassigned elsewhere in the diplomatic service, but layoffs are expected, it added.

Two sources familiar with the situation told Reuters the leadership of the mission to China had sent out a message to embassy staff "refuting" the report. Embassy spokespeople did not immediately respond to a request for comment and the U.S. State Department had no immediate comment.

President Donald Trump's administration has asked U.S. embassies worldwide to prepare for staff cuts, sources told Reuters last week, as part of the Republican president's effort to overhaul the U.S. diplomatic corps.

Trump and his billionaire ally Elon Musk have been making efforts since Trump took office on January 20 to cut U.S. government spending they consider wasteful.

The United States has a large diplomatic workforce in China and those in Beijing are located in a 10-acre site consisting of six buildings, including an annex completed in 2016.

According to the website of the U.S. Embassy and Consulates in China, the Beijing embassy complex houses more than 1,300 American and locally hired staff representing almost 50 different U.S. federal agencies.

data.title: New York Times business news - February 20
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the New York Times business pages. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- Apple has unveiled the iPhone 16e, priced at $599, featuring AI capabilities called Apple Intelligence, and replacing the iPhone SE as the company's most affordable model.

- Nikola , the electric truck maker, has filed for bankruptcy protection, struggling with financial and operational challenges despite initial investor enthusiasm and efforts to produce hydrogen-powered trucks.

- Microsoft has developed a new state of matter to create "topological qubits" for quantum computers, potentially revolutionizing technology by solving complex problems more efficiently than classical computers.

- KFC is relocating its corporate headquarters from Kentucky to Texas, ending decades of association with its home state, to enhance collaboration among Yum Brands' companies and benefit from Texas' business-friendly environment.

data.title: Forbo CEO Falls Ill, Hands Over Operational Management to CFO
data.description:
Forbo said Thursday that Chief Executive Officer Jens Fankhänel has fallen sick.

While Fankhänel is recovering, the operational management of the Swiss producer of floor coverings, building, and construction adhesives was handed over to Chief Financial Officer Andreas Jaeger.

An update is expected in mid-April.

data.title: iPhone supplier Murata eyeing large-scale M&A to drive growth, CEO says
data.description:
Japan's Murata Manufacturing is considering mergers and acquisitions (M&A) larger than 100 billion yen ($665 million) to drive growth, the smartphone component supplier's CEO said on Thursday.

Murata is considering areas such as inductors and sensors with overseas targets also a possibility as the company seeks to grow its market share and expand in new markets, Norio Nakajima said.

"We are taking a broad view," Nakajima told Reuters in an interview at the company's headquarters in Kyoto.

($1 = 150.2600 yen)

data.title: Leejam Sports Launches New Women's Fitness Center in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Leejam Sports (SASE:1830) opened a new fitness center for women in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, on Wednesday.

The Saudi Arabian sports and fitness center operator's new site will run under the Fitness Time - Ladies brand, according to a Thursday release.
